registry_refs.notes: Registry rank 109 contains both harvardjlg.com and journals.law.harvard.edu/jlg rows.
Existing sitemap records the Harvard Journals host as the canonical working archive.

publication_scope.notes: The user-supplied harvardjlg.com host is currently HTTP Basic-auth protected; the existing registry's Harvard Journals route is public and journal-scoped.

evidence.notes: Returned HTTP Basic authentication challenge; no further requests were made to this host.
Allows public paths, disallows /wp-admin/ except admin-ajax.php, and specifies Crawl-delay: 10.

Single WordPress page has 43 volume/issue headings from volume 47 (2024) back to volume 26 (2003), 109 direct PDF links, and row text combining title, author(s), and first page. Several older rows lack PDF links.

reconnaissance.notes: The protected vanity host is a blocker only for that route. The already-recorded canonical Harvard Journals archive is public and structurally rich, but no PDF was fetched after the bounded preflight.

exclude: Other Harvard journals on the shared host
contents, boards, and front matter unless separately classified
scope.notes: Keep the /jlg/ prefix and sites/88 upload directory as independent scope guards.

entry_points.notes: Preferred single-page direct-PDF archive; obey the host's ten-second crawl delay.

nodes.notes: One page containing volume sections and article rows.
h2 heading followed by a table of works.
Most linked works go directly to WordPress PDF uploads.

edges.notes: Heading text encodes volume, optional issue, season, and year, with inconsistent case and punctuation.
The first cell's anchor supplies title and file URL; remaining row text supplies authors and the second cell supplies starting page.

archive_patterns.notes: Upload year is not necessarily publication year; older files also use legacy law.harvard.edu URLs.

pagination.termination: Single page ends at volume 26, Spring 2003.

layout_eras.label: WordPress table archive with direct PDFs
layout_eras.notes: A single page includes newest, middle, and oldest sections; link coverage becomes incomplete in older rows.

sources.example: Online Shaming and the Power of Informal Justice
sources.notes: Rows without links still retain styled title text.

title.missingness: Visible for sampled newest, middle, and oldest rows.

sources.locator: figure.wp-block-table tr td:first-child text after the title anchor
sources.notes: Parse the trailing byline conservatively; markup is not separately classed.

authors.missingness: Authored works expose names; contents/front matter do not.

abstract.missingness: The direct-PDF archive provides no abstracts and no article landing pages were observed.
abstract.notes: PDF-derived abstracts may be possible later.

sources.notes: Extract four-digit year from issue heading, not upload path.

sources.locator: nearest preceding h2.wp-block-heading season and year
sources.notes: Season is less precise than a date.

date.missingness: Season is present on most sampled headings.

sources.locator: optional suffix after volume number in h2 heading
sources.notes: Some headings give only a volume and season.

issue.missingness: Not explicit for volume 47 and several other headings.

sources.notes: Appears to be starting page only.

doi.missingness: No DOI observed on the archive.

citation.notes: Can be assembled from volume, year, and starting page after journal abbreviation policy is defined.

patterns.notes: 109 direct PDF hrefs were observed, but no file request was made in this bounded pass.

validation.notes: PDF delivery and granularity remain unverified.

robots.notes: HTTP 401 prevented reading rules; stop this host.

barriers.notes: The user-supplied vanity host requires HTTP Basic authentication.
Canonical host requires a ten-second crawl delay.

access.politeness_notes: The protected vanity host was stopped after robots returned 401. The canonical archive was requested only after its robots check and ten-second delay.

verdict.summary: The requested vanity host is login-protected, but the public canonical Harvard Journals WordPress page exposes an unpaginated volume archive with direct article-PDF links and row-level title, author, year, volume, issue, and starting-page metadata.
blockers: harvardjlg.com robots and content are behind HTTP Basic authentication.
No representative PDF smoke was performed.
Older archive rows have incomplete PDF-link coverage and no abstracts.
verdict.next_action: Use the canonical /jlg/past-volumes/ route with Crawl-delay 10, run one direct-PDF smoke, and build a table-row parser that records unlinked older works as metadata-only gaps.
verdict.recommended_adapter: WordPressAcademicBaseAdapter with JLG past-volumes table parser
verdict.implementation_notes: Do not use harvardjlg.com while protected. Segment rows by preceding h2 volume heading; parse title from the first-cell anchor, authors from trailing cell text, first page from the second cell, and filter contents/board records.
